feed.title: French car parts maker Faurecia opens $147 mln plant in Mexico-Faurecia said it expects the plant in the northern border state of Nuevo Leon, serving clients su
feed.summary: French car parts maker Faurecia opens $147 mln plant in Mexico-Faurecia said it expects the plant in the northern border state of Nuevo Leon, serving clients such as Volvo VOLCARb.ST and Stellantis STLAM.MI, to produce more than 2 million vehicles' worth of car seats a year by 2025. Of the 125 high-level investments to land in Nuevo Leon in recent years, more than a quarter belong to the auto sector, state Economy Minister Ivan Rivas said in a statement. Moves to Mexico are part of the "nearshoring" trend aimed at bringing operations from Asia closer to their final destination, which tends to be the United States.

feed.title: EXCLUSIVE-Indonesian president pledges nickel mining clean up amid EV-led boom-The development of the electric vehicle (EV) sector in Indonesia is a personal mi
feed.summary: EXCLUSIVE-Indonesian president pledges nickel mining clean up amid EV-led boom-The development of the electric vehicle (EV) sector in Indonesia is a personal mission for Jokowi, who has been trying to convince Tesla TSLA.O CEO Elon Musk to manufacture EVs or batteries in the sprawling Southeast Asian archipelago. HARARE, March 30 (Reuters) - Syrah Resources SYR.AX achieved record graphite production in 2022 and its Vidalia active anode plant in the U.S is on course for completion by the third quarter of this year, the Australia-based company said on Thursday. The plant positions Syrah as an alternative supplier of graphite anode material to the U.S and Europe, which currently rely on supplies from China.

feed.summary: Tesla faces new race bias claims while a separate trial wraps up-By Daniel Wiessner March 31 (Reuters) - Tesla Inc TSLA.O has been sued by a Black former general manager who says he was fired for pushing back against comments by his white supervisor that he claims were racist, the latest in a series of race bias cases against the world's most valuable automaker. feed.title: Tesla's price war: cheaper cars expected to drive record sales-By Hyunjoo Jin and Akash Sriram SAN FRANCISCO, March 31 (Reuters) - Tesla Inc TSLA.O investors ar
feed.summary: Tesla's price war: cheaper cars expected to drive record sales-By Hyunjoo Jin and Akash Sriram SAN FRANCISCO, March 31 (Reuters) - Tesla Inc TSLA.O investors are waiting to see how much Elon Musk's price cuts will pay off: the electric vehicle (EV) maker is expected this weekend to report record sales as cheaper sticker prices, helped by U.S. subsidies, spurred demand. Musk's action in January kicked off a price war that was especially heated in China, betting that Tesla's industry-leading profit margins would let it rebuff growing competition and appease customers in a weak economy. "With Tesla likely to continue ramping production at both Austin and Berlin, additional supply is likely to drive further price cuts," Barclays analyst Dan Levy said.

feed.title: US judge in Tesla race bias lawsuit denies plaintiff's bid for mistrial-By Daniel Wiessner March 31 (Reuters) - A Black former Tesla Inc TSLA.O factory worker s
feed.summary: US judge in Tesla race bias lawsuit denies plaintiff's bid for mistrial-By Daniel Wiessner March 31 (Reuters) - A Black former Tesla Inc TSLA.O factory worker suing the electric-vehicle maker for race discrimination lost his bid for a mistrial on Friday after claiming the company's lawyers violated court rules by trying to turn jurors against him. U.S. District Judge William Orrick denied the motion during a pretrial conference, saying Diaz had not shown that comments by Tesla's lawyers had prejudiced the jury. But Orrick agreed with Tesla's lawyers that the questioning of Diaz was proper because his alleged comments were related to incidents that were discussed during the 2021 trial.

feed.title: Mexico sees growth of up to 3% this year, eyes nearshoring boost-Electric vehicle maker Tesla TSLA.O recently announced it would build a "gigafactory" in the no
feed.summary: Mexico sees growth of up to 3% this year, eyes nearshoring boost-Electric vehicle maker Tesla TSLA.O recently announced it would build a "gigafactory" in the northern border state of Nuevo Leon, which local officials have said could bring in up to $10 billion in investment and create 10,000 jobs. The ministry estimates Latin America's second-biggest economy will expand between 2.2% and 3.0% this year, and between 1.6% and 3.0% in 2024, the document showed, as the country continues to claw back pandemic-led losses. The Dos Bocas refinery, a Lopez Obrador project underway in the Gulf state of Tabasco, is expected to begin operating at full capacity next year, the finance ministry said.

feed.title: Elon Musk's 2018 tweet on Tesla union campaign illegal, US court rules-By Daniel Wiessner March 31 (Reuters) - A U.S. appeals court said on Friday Tesla Inc TSL
feed.summary: Elon Musk's 2018 tweet on Tesla union campaign illegal, US court rules-By Daniel Wiessner March 31 (Reuters) - A U.S. appeals court said on Friday Tesla Inc TSLA.O CEO Elon Musk violated federal labor law by tweeting that employees of the electric vehicle maker would lose stock options if they joined a union. Circuit Court of Appeals upheld a decision by the U.S. National Labor Relations Board that said the 2018 tweet amounted to an unlawful threat that could discourage unionizing and ordered Musk to delete it. "Substantial evidence supports the NLRB’s conclusion that the tweet is an implied threat to end stock options as retaliation for unionization," the 5th Circuit panel wrote.

feed.title: Elon Musk seeks to end $258 billion Dogecoin lawsuit-In an evening filing in Manhattan federal court, lawyers for Musk and his electric car company Tesla Inc TS
feed.summary: Elon Musk seeks to end $258 billion Dogecoin lawsuit-In an evening filing in Manhattan federal court, lawyers for Musk and his electric car company Tesla Inc TSLA.O called the lawsuit by Dogecoin investors a "fanciful work of fiction" over Musk's "innocuous and often silly tweets" about Dogecoin. By Jonathan Stempel NEW YORK, March 31 (Reuters) - Elon Musk asked a U.S. judge on Friday to throw out a $258 billion racketeering lawsuit accusing him of running a pyramid scheme to support the cryptocurrency Dogecoin. The lawyers said the investors never explained how Musk intended to defraud anyone or what risks he concealed, and that his statements such as "Dogecoin Rulz" and "no highs, no lows, only Doge" were too vague to support a fraud claim.

feed.summary: US unveils stricter EV tax credit rules to take effect April 18-Tesla TSLA.Osaid Wednesday the Model 3 rear-wheel drive credit will be reduced as a result of the guidance. They included a North American assembly requirement from August, price and buyer income eligibility caps from Jan. 1, and now the battery and critical minerals sourcing rules, effective April 18. feed.summary: US STOCKS-S&P 500 ends higher as oil stocks rally; Tesla tumbles-Tesla Inc TSLA.O dropped after disclosing March-quarter deliveries rose just 4% from the previous quarter, even after CEO Elon Musk slashed car prices in January to boost demand. By Noel Randewich and Ankika Biswas April 3 (Reuters) - The S&P 500 ended higher on Monday, lifted by energy stocks following surprise cuts to the OPEC+ group's oil output targets, while Tesla tumbled after its electric vehicle deliveries for the first quarter disappointed investors. However, the prospect of higher oil costs added to inflation worries on Wall Street just days after evidence of cooling prices raised expectations that the U.S. Federal Reserve might soon end its aggressive monetary tightening campaign.

feed.summary: US STOCKS-S&P 500, Nasdaq fall as inflation worries resurface; Tesla slides-Tesla Inc TSLA.O shed 6.1% on growing worries about the electric-vehicle maker's profit margins after aggressive price cuts led to only a modest increase in quarterly deliveries. By Ankika Biswas and Amruta Khandekar April 3 (Reuters) - The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq fell on Monday as rising oil prices rekindled concerns that the U.S. Federal Reserve will stick to its rate-hike campaign for longer to temper inflation, while a slump in Tesla shares also pressured the benchmark index. A 3.9% gain in energy major Chevron Corp CVX.N on the jump in oil prices and a 3.6% rise in UnitedHealth Group Inc UNH.N on better-than-proposed Medicare Advantage rates for 2024, helped stem losses on the S&P 500 and assisted the price-weighted Dow Jones in outshining its peers.

A 3.9% gain in energy major Chevron Corp CVX.N on the jump in oil prices and a 3.6% rise in UnitedHealth Group Inc UNH.N on better-than-proposed Medicare Advantage rates for 2024, helped stem losses on the S&P 500 and assisted the price-weighted Dow Jones in outshining its peers.

feed.title: Consumer Sector Update for 04/03/2023: EDR, WWE, TSLA, GM-Tesla (TSLA) was down over 7% after saying it produced 440,808 vehicles in Q1, up from 305,407 units a
feed.summary: Consumer Sector Update for 04/03/2023: EDR, WWE, TSLA, GM-Tesla (TSLA) was down over 7% after saying it produced 440,808 vehicles in Q1, up from 305,407 units a year earlier. In company news, Endeavor Group (EDR) and World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE) have agreed to merge to form a new publicly traded company that will be 51% owned by Endeavour and 49% by existing WWE's shareholders. General Motors (GM) said it sold 603,208 vehicles in the US in the first quarter, up 18% from a year earlier.

feed.title: Why Canoo, QuantumScape, and Lucid Group Stocks Dropped Today-What happened Monday dawned darkly for investors in stocks tied to the electric car industry, as i
feed.summary: Why Canoo, QuantumScape, and Lucid Group Stocks Dropped Today-What happened Monday dawned darkly for investors in stocks tied to the electric car industry, as investors in EV start-up Canoo (NASDAQ: GOEV), solid-state battery researcher QuantumScape (NYSE: QS) and luxury EV maker Lucid Group (NASDAQ: LCID) all reacted negatively to news of Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA)'s Q1 2023 "deliveries miss." Despite missing expectations, Tesla might still have siphoned up EV demand that would otherwise go to rivals like Canoo and Lucid -- in which case their upcoming results could be even worse than feared. And seeming to confirm that fear, Lucid has announced plans to lay off 18% of its workforce, while Canoo just last week reported that it sold just one single vehicle -- to the U.S. Army -- last quarter.

feed.title: US STOCKS-Wall Street mixed following inflation fears; Tesla tumbles-Tesla Inc TSLA.O dropped 6.8% after disclosing March-quarter deliveries rose just 4% from t
feed.summary: US STOCKS-Wall Street mixed following inflation fears; Tesla tumbles-Tesla Inc TSLA.O dropped 6.8% after disclosing March-quarter deliveries rose just 4% from the previous quarter, even after CEO Elon Musk slashed car prices in January to boost demand. By Noel Randewich and Ankika Biswas April 3 (Reuters) - Wall Street was mixed on Monday as rising oil prices rekindled worries about interest rate hikes, while Tesla tumbled after its electric vehicle deliveries for the first quarter disappointed investors. The prospect of higher oil costs added to inflation worries on Wall Street just days after evidence of cooling prices raised expectations that the U.S. Federal Reserve might soon end its aggressive monetary tightening campaign.

feed.title: US STOCKS-S&P 500 ends higher as oil stocks rally; Tesla tumbles-Tesla Inc TSLA.O dropped 6.1% after disclosing March-quarter deliveries rose just 4% from the p
feed.summary: US STOCKS-S&P 500 ends higher as oil stocks rally; Tesla tumbles-Tesla Inc TSLA.O dropped 6.1% after disclosing March-quarter deliveries rose just 4% from the previous quarter, even after CEO Elon Musk slashed car prices in January to boost demand. By Noel Randewich and Ankika Biswas April 3 (Reuters) - The S&P 500 ended higher on Monday, lifted by energy stocks following surprise cuts to the OPEC+ group's oil output targets, while Tesla tumbled after its electric vehicle deliveries for the first quarter disappointed investors. However, the prospect of higher oil costs added to inflation worries on Wall Street just days after evidence of cooling prices raised expectations that the U.S. Federal Reserve might soon end its aggressive monetary tightening campaign.

feed.title: FOREX-Dollar skids after soft U.S. economic data; impact of OPEC+ cuts fades-In cryptocurrencies, Dogecoin, a meme coin supported by Tesla Inc TSLA.O founder El
feed.summary: FOREX-Dollar skids after soft U.S. economic data; impact of OPEC+ cuts fades-In cryptocurrencies, Dogecoin, a meme coin supported by Tesla Inc TSLA.O founder Elon Musk, soared 27% on Monday to $0.10 DOGE=KRKN after Twitter's webpage used the token's dog icon instead of the social media website's usual blue bird, market participants said. By Gertrude Chavez-Dreyfuss NEW YORK, April 3 (Reuters) - The dollar stumbled on Monday, surrendering earlier gains following unexpected oil output cuts from OPEC+, as data showed the U.S. economy continued to slow with declines in manufacturing and construction spending. An announcement on Sunday of output target cuts by the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) and its allies, known as OPEC+, propelled oil prices higher.

feed.title: US sales at top automakers rise on improving inventory, Toyota struggles-EV leader Tesla Inc TSLA.O posted record deliveries but its shares fell on Monday on gr
feed.summary: US sales at top automakers rise on improving inventory, Toyota struggles-EV leader Tesla Inc TSLA.O posted record deliveries but its shares fell on Monday on growing margin worries after aggressive price cuts. Vehicle production took a hit after the pandemic disrupted supply of semiconductor chips and other raw materials, hurting carmakers' ability to meet the upsurge in demand for personal mobility. But rising interest rates and fears of a recession may play spoilsport in an industry where most vehicle purchases are financed with loans, analysts say, as they watch out for signs of plateauing demand.

feed.title: GLOBAL MARKETS-Brent crude jumps $5/bbl after output cuts; stocks rise-Shares of Tesla Inc TSLA.O dropped 6.1% after disclosing March-quarter deliveries rose ju
feed.summary: GLOBAL MARKETS-Brent crude jumps $5/bbl after output cuts; stocks rise-Shares of Tesla Inc TSLA.O dropped 6.1% after disclosing March-quarter deliveries rose just 4% from the previous quarter, even after CEO Elon Musk slashed car prices in January to boost demand. By Caroline Valetkevitch NEW YORK, April 3 (Reuters) - Brent crude oil climbed $5 a barrel on Monday after Saudi Arabia and other OPEC+ producers announced new production cuts, and gains in energy shares helped lift world stock indexes. Investors also digested Monday's economic data, which showed U.S. manufacturing activity in March slumped to its lowest level in nearly three years as new orders continued to contract.

feed.title: Jury orders Tesla to pay $3.2 mln to Black ex-worker in US race bias case-By Daniel Wiessner April 3 (Reuters) - A federal jury in San Francisco on Monday order
feed.summary: Jury orders Tesla to pay $3.2 mln to Black ex-worker in US race bias case-By Daniel Wiessner April 3 (Reuters) - A federal jury in San Francisco on Monday ordered Tesla Inc TSLA.O to pay about $3.2 million to a Black former employee after the electric-vehicle maker was found to have failed to prevent severe racial harassment at its flagship assembly plant in California. Bernard Alexander, a lawyer for Diaz, urged jurors during closing statements on Friday to award him nearly $160 million in damages, and send a message to Tesla and other large companies that they will be held accountable for failing to address discrimination. They claimed Tesla's legal team violated Orrick's bar on introducing new evidence in the retrial by questioning Diaz and other witnesses about incidents where he allegedly made racist or sexual comments.

feed.title: Tesla shares slip as margin worries overshadow record deliveries-Adds graphic; updates shares April 3 (Reuters) - Tesla Inc TSLA.O shares fell 5% on Monday, dra
feed.summary: Tesla shares slip as margin worries overshadow record deliveries-Adds graphic; updates shares April 3 (Reuters) - Tesla Inc TSLA.O shares fell 5% on Monday, dragged down by growing worries about the electric-vehicle maker's profit margins after aggressive price cuts led to only a modest increase in quarterly deliveries. Several analysts said the figures raised questions about whether more price cuts would be needed this year to achieve CEO Elon Musk's target of 2 million deliveries for 2023. "We maintain that price cuts have and will undermine industry profitability (including Tesla's), but that incumbents are deep pocketed and not likely to back down," they added.

"We maintain that price cuts have and will undermine industry profitability (including Tesla's), but that incumbents are deep pocketed and not likely to back down," they added.

An uptick in U.S. Treasury yields pushed major technology stocks and other growth shares such as Apple Inc AAPL.O, Amazon.com Inc AMZN.O, Microsoft Corp MSFT.O and Alphabet Inc GOOGL.O down between 0.6% and 0.9%. feed.summary: US STOCKS-S&P, Nasdaq futures slip as oil output cut reignites inflation worries-Among other major stocks, Tesla Inc TSLA.O fell 2.1% after the electric-vehicle maker posted record quarterly vehicle deliveries, but quarter-on-quarter sales growth was modest despite price cuts. By Ankika Biswas and Amruta Khandekar April 3 (Reuters) - Futures tracking the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq fell on Monday as a surge in oil prices renewed worries of persistent inflationary pressures, bolstering bets that the U.S. Federal Reserve will deliver another interest rate hike at its next meeting. O/R "We could see inflation bottom out a little bit higher than anticipated, which may mean that the Fed continues their rate hiking a lot longer and further than many currently expect," said Paul Nolte, senior wealth adviser and market strategist at Murphy & Sylvest.

By Ankika Biswas and Amruta Khandekar April 3 (Reuters) - Futures tracking the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq fell on Monday as a surge in oil prices renewed worries of persistent inflationary pressures, bolstering bets that the U.S. Federal Reserve will deliver another interest rate hike at its next meeting. O/R "We could see inflation bottom out a little bit higher than anticipated, which may mean that the Fed continues their rate hiking a lot longer and further than many currently expect," said Paul Nolte, senior wealth adviser and market strategist at Murphy & Sylvest.

feed.title: Tesla Deliveries Disappoint, but This Mining Stock Is Shining Bright-One stock that dragged on Nasdaq futures was Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA), which reported its lates
feed.summary: Tesla Deliveries Disappoint, but This Mining Stock Is Shining Bright-One stock that dragged on Nasdaq futures was Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA), which reported its latest delivery and production numbers over the weekend. The electric vehicle pioneer reported its first-quarter figures for production and delivery volumes, and investors seemed more worried about the future pace of growth than they were pleased to see sizable gains from past periods. The mining company reported that it rejected a would-be acquisition offer, but that still had shareholders speculating about whether Teck might accept a more lucrative bid if it came in the near future.

feed.title: Tesla shares fall as margin worries overshadow record deliveries-April 3 (Reuters) - Tesla Inc TSLA.O shares fell about 4% on Monday, dragged down by growing wo
feed.summary: Tesla shares fall as margin worries overshadow record deliveries-April 3 (Reuters) - Tesla Inc TSLA.O shares fell about 4% on Monday, dragged down by growing worries about the electric-vehicle maker's profit margins after aggressive price cuts led to only a modest increase in quarterly deliveries. Several analysts said the figures raised questions about whether more price cuts would be needed this year to achieve CEO Elon Musk's target of 2 million deliveries for 2023. "We maintain that price cuts have and will undermine industry profitability (including Tesla's), but that incumbents are deep pocketed and not likely to back down," they added.

feed.title: US STOCKS-S&P 500, Nasdaq set to open lower as oil output cut stokes inflation worries-By Ankika Biswas and Amruta Khandekar April 3 (Reuters) - The S&P 500 and
feed.summary: US STOCKS-S&P 500, Nasdaq set to open lower as oil output cut stokes inflation worries-By Ankika Biswas and Amruta Khandekar April 3 (Reuters) - The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq were set to open lower on Monday as rising oil prices brought back inflation worries and fueled bets of another interest rate hike by the Federal Reserve in its next meeting. "We could see inflation bottom out a little bit higher than anticipated, which may mean that the Fed continues their rate hiking a lot longer and further than many currently expect," said Paul Nolte, senior wealth adviser and market strategist at Murphy & Sylvest. Major technology stocks and other growth shares such as Apple Inc AAPL.O, Amazon.com Inc AMZN.O, Microsoft Corp MSFT.O and Alphabet Inc GOOGL.O fell between 0.5% and 1.1% in premarket trade tracking higher U.S. Treasury yields.

"We could see inflation bottom out a little bit higher than anticipated, which may mean that the Fed continues their rate hiking a lot longer and further than many currently expect," said Paul Nolte, senior wealth adviser and market strategist at Murphy & Sylvest. Major technology stocks and other growth shares such as Apple Inc AAPL.O, Amazon.com Inc AMZN.O, Microsoft Corp MSFT.O and Alphabet Inc GOOGL.O fell between 0.5% and 1.1% in premarket trade tracking higher U.S. Treasury yields.

feed.title: Dogecoin jumps as Musk's Twitter flips logo to Shiba Inu dog-The Tesla TSLA.O boss on Friday asked a U.S. judge to throw out a $258 billion racketeering lawsuit
feed.summary: Dogecoin jumps as Musk's Twitter flips logo to Shiba Inu dog-The Tesla TSLA.O boss on Friday asked a U.S. judge to throw out a $258 billion racketeering lawsuit accusing him of running a pyramid scheme to support dogecoin. Musk, who is ranked the second-richest person in the world by Forbes, is a vocal proponent of cryptocurrencies and has heavily influenced prices for dogecoin and bitcoin in the past. "A year later, Twitter has yet to announce any crypto specific initiatives," said Stéphane Ouellette, Chief Executive at digital asset investment platform FRNT Financial FRNT.V.

feed.summary: Why Some Investors May Be Disappointed in Tesla's Deliveries-Electric-car maker Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) announced some impressive vehicle delivery numbers over the weekend. feed.title: Consumer Sector Update for 04/04/2023: NAPA, TSLA, KIRK-Tesla (TSLA) delivered 88,869 electric vehicles made in China in March, up 35% from a year earlier, Reut
feed.summary: Consumer Sector Update for 04/04/2023: NAPA, TSLA, KIRK-Tesla (TSLA) delivered 88,869 electric vehicles made in China in March, up 35% from a year earlier, Reuters reported Tuesday, citing data from the China Passenger Car Association. Kikland's (KIRK) rose 4.2% despite reporting a fiscal Q4 adjusted loss of $0.09 per diluted share, swinging from earnings of $0.84 a year earlier. Additionally, the company said Chief Executive Officer Steve Woodward will retire, effective May 31.
feed.raw_text: Consumer Sector Update for 04/04/2023: NAPA, TSLA, KIRK-Tesla (TSLA) delivered 88,869 electric vehicles made in China in March, up 35% from a year earlier, Reuters reported Tuesday, citing data from the China Passenger Car Association. Kikland's (KIRK) rose 4.2% despite reporting a fiscal Q4 adjusted loss of $0.09 per diluted share, swinging from earnings of $0.84 a year earlier. Additionally, the company said Chief Executive Officer Steve Woodward will retire, effective May 31.

feed.title: QQQ Stocks Drove S&P 500 Q1 Gains-Tesla (TSLA), which is the second-largest consumer discretionary holding in QQQ and QQQM, was another major contributor to the
feed.summary: QQQ Stocks Drove S&P 500 Q1 Gains-Tesla (TSLA), which is the second-largest consumer discretionary holding in QQQ and QQQM, was another major contributor to the S&P 500’s first-quarter run, despite facing intensifying competition. “A key driver for NVDA is that the company already has a vast install base in which they can tap into to expand adoption of their AI solutions,” wrote Piper Sandler analyst Harsh Kumar in a recent report. “By starting with the current install base, this creates a customer feedback loop that should accelerate AI adoption.” In what could signal a sympathy trade, Nvidia rival Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) posted a first-quarter gain of about 51%, benefiting QQQ and QQQM because the ETFs feature a 1.20% weight to that chip stock.

feed.title: US STOCKS-Futures edge higher as tech and growth stocks gain; focus on jobs data-Tesla Inc TSLA.O rose 1.6% in premarket trading, bouncing back from 6% declines
feed.summary: US STOCKS-Futures edge higher as tech and growth stocks gain; focus on jobs data-Tesla Inc TSLA.O rose 1.6% in premarket trading, bouncing back from 6% declines on Monday after disclosing March-quarter deliveries, while Meta Platforms Inc META.O and Microsoft Corp MSFT.O climbed about 0.6% each. O/R The renewed inflation worries poured cold water on expectations that the Fed may soon end its aggressive policy tightening amid recent signs of cooling prices, with fresh data on Monday pointing to weakening U.S. manufacturing activity. Among stocks, Virgin Orbit Holdings Inc VORB.O tanked 25% after the satellite launch company filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy on failing to secure long-term funding.

feed.title: MORNING BID AMERICAS-Markets brush off OPEC as factories stall-Big Tech and Tesla were the main losers on Monday, with Tesla TSLA.O losing more than 6% after di
feed.summary: MORNING BID AMERICAS-Markets brush off OPEC as factories stall-Big Tech and Tesla were the main losers on Monday, with Tesla TSLA.O losing more than 6% after disclosing underwhelming March-quarter deliveries despite slashing car prices in January. Consensus forecasts for job creation last month show only a modest easing from the monster February reading, but PIMCO points out that the wave of corporate layoffs announced in January probably won't register fully until April due to 60-day notice periods for employees of large firms. The Australian dollar went in the other direction, however, and reversed much of Monday's gain as the Reserve Bank of Australia left its cash rate unchanged at 3.6% to break a run of 10 straight hikes, saying it wanted more time to assess the impact of past increases.
feed.raw_text: MORNING BID AMERICAS-Markets brush off OPEC as factories stall-Big Tech and Tesla were the main losers on Monday, with Tesla TSLA.O losing more than 6% after disclosing underwhelming March-quarter deliveries despite slashing car prices in January. Consensus forecasts for job creation last month show only a modest easing from the monster February reading, but PIMCO points out that the wave of corporate layoffs announced in January probably won't register fully until April due to 60-day notice periods for employees of large firms. The Australian dollar went in the other direction, however, and reversed much of Monday's gain as the Reserve Bank of Australia left its cash rate unchanged at 3.6% to break a run of 10 straight hikes, saying it wanted more time to assess the impact of past increases.

feed.title: Tesla delivers 88,869 China-made EVs in March - CPCA-By comparison, BYD 1211.HK sold 206,089 units last month with its Dynasty and Ocean series of EVs and hybri
feed.summary: Tesla delivers 88,869 China-made EVs in March - CPCA-By comparison, BYD 1211.HK sold 206,089 units last month with its Dynasty and Ocean series of EVs and hybrids, up 97.5% from a year ago, CPCA data showed. Globally, Tesla posted record quarterly vehicle deliveries in the January to March period, but quarter-on-quarter sales growth was modest despite price cuts as rising competition and a bleak economic outlook weighed. Tesla's retail sales in China are poised to show the best quarter in the first three months, data from China Merchants Bank International(CMBI) showed, totalled 122,801 units as of March 26 and accounting for 13% in China's new energy car sales, which includes both pure electric and plug-in hybrid cars.

feed.title: Bill Gates says calls to pause AI won't 'solve challenges'-His interview with Reuters comes after an open letter -- published last week and co-signed by Elon Mu
feed.summary: Bill Gates says calls to pause AI won't 'solve challenges'-His interview with Reuters comes after an open letter -- published last week and co-signed by Elon Musk and more than 1,000 AI experts – demanded an urgent pause in the development of systems "more powerful" than Microsoft-backed MSFT.O OpenAI's new GPT-4, which can hold human-like conversation, compose songs and summarise lengthy documents. The experts, including Apple AAPL.O co-founder Steve Wozniak, said in the letter the potential risks and benefits to society need to be assessed. “Clearly there’s huge benefits to these things… what we need to do is identify the tricky areas.” Microsoft has sought to outpace peers through multi-billion-dollar investments in ChatGPT owner OpenAI.

feed.summary: 3 Top Industries to Invest In After the Fed Pause-While Tesla (TSLA), Rivian (RIVN), Nio (NIO), XPeng (XPEV), and others released positive updates, their stocks dropped in response. feed.summary: US STOCKS-S&P 500 ends lower as recession fears take center stage-Tesla Inc TSLA.O, Amazon AMZN.O, Apple AAPL.O and Microsoft MSFT.O all declined, pulling down the Nasdaq and reversing gains in some of Wall Street's most valuable companies in recent weeks. By Noel Randewich and Ankika Biswas April 5 (Reuters) - The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq ended lower on Wednesday after a growing wave of weak economic data deepened worries that the Federal Reserve's rapid interest rate hikes might tip the U.S. economy into a recession. Nvidia Corp NVDA.O was among the stocks weighing most on the S&P 500 after Alphabet Inc's GOOGL.O Google unit said the supercomputers it uses to train its artificial intelligence models were faster and more power-efficient than comparable components made by the chipmaker.

feed.title: Invest with (or Against) Jim Cramer with These New ETFs-Looking at LJIM as a whole, you’ll find an assortment of blue-chip U.S. stocks ranging from Dow componen
feed.summary: Invest with (or Against) Jim Cramer with These New ETFs-Looking at LJIM as a whole, you’ll find an assortment of blue-chip U.S. stocks ranging from Dow components like Boeing (NYSE:BA) and Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT) to more tech and growth names like Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD), Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA), and the aforementioned Nvidia and Meta Platforms. Like Cramer, ARK’s founder and CIO Cathie Wood is another high-profile, polarizing figure in the investing world, so it appears that Tuttle is going back to the well here with these two Cramer-themed ETFs. Cramer is well-known to be a long-time fan of Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), even going as far as to name his dog after the company, so it is unsurprising that the semiconductor giant is LJIM’s largest holding, with a 5% weighting.

feed.summary: US STOCKS-Wall St eyes subdued open as private payrolls data deepens recession worries-As U.S. Treasury yields dropped after the data, major technology and growth stocks such as Meta Platforms Inc META.O, Tesla Inc TSLA.O and Amazon.com Inc AMZN.O eked out gains in premarket trade. feed.summary: Consumer Discretionary ETFs: Luxury Leisurely Takes Lead-The top performers in XLY include Tesla (TSLA), which is up 56.3% YTD, and several travel stocks including Wynn Resorts (WYNN), MGM Resorts (MGM), and Booking Holdings (BKNG) which are benefiting from continuing demand for “revenge travel” post-pandemic. feed.summary: FOCUS-Toyota looks to overhaul EV strategy as new CEO takes charge-The world's biggest automaker increasingly recognises it needs to match Tesla Inc's TSLA.O design and manufacturing innovations if it is to drive down production costs and turn its all-electric business into a higher margin one as its Silicon Valley rival has done, another person said. By Norihiko Shirouzu, Joseph White and Maki Shiraki April 6 (Reuters) - After making sweeping changes to its leadership team, Japan's Toyota Motor Corp 7203.T is looking at a factory floor overhaul as it maps out a move to a new, dedicated platform for battery electric vehicles, four people familiar with the matter said. Sato, Toyoda's hand-picked successor, recently attended an internal presentation that focused on the need for a dedicated battery-electric platform, a more competitive system to manage heat generated by the battery as well as other innovations influenced by Tesla's playbook, according to a third person.

feed.title: US STOCKS-Wall St falls as recession fears rise after jobless claims data-Major technology and growth stocks such as Apple Inc AAPL.O, Tesla Inc TSLA.O and Nvid
feed.summary: US STOCKS-Wall St falls as recession fears rise after jobless claims data-Major technology and growth stocks such as Apple Inc AAPL.O, Tesla Inc TSLA.O and Nvidia Corp NVDA.O fell between 0.9% and 2.1% in early trading, while bond yields inched higher. A string of recent reports, including weak data on private payrolls and job openings earlier this week, have suggested slowing labor demand and raised hopes of a pause in the Federal Reserve's market-punishing rate hikes. However, unlike in the last few months when evidence of a cooling economy was cheered by investors on hopes it would allow for a less hawkish Fed, softer data has added to fears of a recession and pressured equities in recent days.

feed.summary: FOCUS-Toyota looks to overhaul EV strategy as new CEO takes charge-The world's biggest automaker increasingly recognises it needs to match Tesla Inc's TSLA.O design and manufacturing innovations if it is to drive down production costs and turn its all-electric business into a higher margin one as its Silicon Valley rival has done, another person said. By Norihiko Shirouzu, Joseph White and Maki Shiraki April 6 (Reuters) - After making sweeping changes to its leadership team, Japan's Toyota Motor Corp 7203.T is looking at a factory floor overhaul as it maps out a move to a new, dedicated platform for battery electric vehicles, four people familiar with the matter said. Sato, Toyoda's hand-picked successor, recently attended an internal presentation that focused on the need for a dedicated battery-electric platform, a more competitive system to manage heat generated by the battery as well as other innovations influenced by Tesla's playbook, according to a third person.

feed.title: US House committee chair 'concerned' by Tesla deals in China-By Michael Martina and Patricia Zengerle WASHINGTON, April 10 (Reuters) - The leader of a U.S. cong
feed.summary: US House committee chair 'concerned' by Tesla deals in China-By Michael Martina and Patricia Zengerle WASHINGTON, April 10 (Reuters) - The leader of a U.S. congressional committee on China said on Monday he was concerned about electric carmaker Tesla Inc's TSLA.O dependency on China, a day after the company revealed plans to open a Megapack battery factory in Shanghai. Tesla announced the factory in a tweet on Sunday, and Chinese state media said it would initially produce 10,000 Megapack units a year, equal to around 40 gigawatt hours of energy storage, and complement a huge existing Shanghai plant making electric vehicles. Tesla's plans to open the Megapack factory come amid growing tensions between China and the U.S. and a push by Beijing to woo foreign firms back after the country's extended COVID-19 lockdowns battered its economy.

feed.title: Sigma Lithium to start production 'within days' after Brazilian approval-The Vancouver-based company should be producing "within days" and shipping lithium "wit
feed.summary: Sigma Lithium to start production 'within days' after Brazilian approval-The Vancouver-based company should be producing "within days" and shipping lithium "within weeks," Ana Cabral-Gardner, Sigma's chief executive, told Reuters. The remaining production is expected to be sold on the spot market to customers that likely will process the metal in China, and Sigma is currently "ascertaining which client would get the first shipment," said Cabral-Gardner. Because lithium demand is low in biofuel-focused Brazil, Cabral-Gardner said Sigma's project is "geared to enable the energy transition in the northern hemisphere."

feed.title: US home solar installers brace for slowdown as California reform looms-By Nichola Groom April 10 (Reuters) - U.S. residential solar installers are bracing for a
feed.summary: US home solar installers brace for slowdown as California reform looms-By Nichola Groom April 10 (Reuters) - U.S. residential solar installers are bracing for a slowdown this year as California, the sector's pioneer and biggest market, is days away from slashing a subsidy for panel owners that has underpinned more than a decade of explosive growth. The policy change, years in the making, will reduce the money credited to rooftop solar owners for sending excess power into the grid, a reform adopted after critics successfully argued the perk is unfair to those without panels. Under the reform, Californians signed up to go solar will be compensated for power their systems send to the grid, at a rate based on what the utility spent to buy clean electricity elsewhere.

The policy change, years in the making, will reduce the money credited to rooftop solar owners for sending excess power into the grid, a reform adopted after critics successfully argued the perk is unfair to those without panels. Under the reform, Californians signed up to go solar will be compensated for power their systems send to the grid, at a rate based on what the utility spent to buy clean electricity elsewhere.

feed.title: US STOCKS-Wall St set for lower open as jobs report stokes rate-hike worries-Tesla Inc TSLA.O fell 2.6% after the electric-vehicle maker cut prices in the Unite
feed.summary: US STOCKS-Wall St set for lower open as jobs report stokes rate-hike worries-Tesla Inc TSLA.O fell 2.6% after the electric-vehicle maker cut prices in the United States between 2% and nearly 6%, a move that analysts cautioned could hurt profitability. By Sruthi Shankar and Ankika Biswas April 10 (Reuters) - U.S. stock indexes were on track for a lower open on Monday on growing concerns that the Federal Reserve will continue to hike interest rates after Friday's jobs data highlighted a still-strong labor market. A string of reports last week, including weak private payrolls and job openings data, pointed to slowing labor demand and raised hopes of the Fed pausing its market-punishing rate hikes amid the recent banking sector turmoil.

feed.title: NIO Could Bring A Pleasant Beat This Coming Quarter-Despite running on lean gross margins since its inception to attract as much market share as possible, manag
feed.summary: NIO Could Bring A Pleasant Beat This Coming Quarter-Despite running on lean gross margins since its inception to attract as much market share as possible, management knows that a price cut would effectively be taken as a negative for their targeted upper-middle-class audience in China who are looking for the status these vehicles can bring them in society. Knowing that the strategy of price cuts for the "common-folk" would not work for NIO vehicles, management has decided to reject the peer pressure and stand their ground. Even though this is a ratio on the richer side, the growth that NIO promises as potential can justify paying a bit of a premium for the company operating in the fastest-growing middle class in the world.

feed.title: Albemarle vs. Sociedad Química y Minera: Which Top Lithium Stock Is the Better Buy?-As is the case with all basic materials and mining companies, the price for
feed.summary: Albemarle vs. Sociedad Química y Minera: Which Top Lithium Stock Is the Better Buy?-As is the case with all basic materials and mining companies, the price for the element -- a key ingredient in batteries for smartphones and electric vehicles (EVs) -- is highly sensitive to even small changes in supply and demand. Nevertheless, by 2027, management believes the volume of lithium it can sell will have increased an average of 20% to 30% a year, all the while maintaining a high level of profitability because of its low-cost operations, primarily in Western Australia and the U.S., and a little from Chile. This puts SQM in a good position to spend on its expansion plans in the coming years, mainly in Chile, but also with a newer venture in Western Australia, to meet rising demand from the EV market.

feed.title: China's car sales stay flat in March amid price war -CPCA-BYD 1211.HK led the segment with market share of 35.5%, while Tesla TSLA.O accounted for 14%. Adds CPC
feed.summary: China's car sales stay flat in March amid price war -CPCA-BYD 1211.HK led the segment with market share of 35.5%, while Tesla TSLA.O accounted for 14%. Adds CPCA official's comments; paragraph 5 BEIJING/SHANGHAI, April 10 (Reuters) - Growth in sales of China's passenger vehicles was flat in March from a year earlier, industry data showed on Monday, as more price cuts by auto brands and the rollout of incentives by local governments helped to support demand. There is no doubt that April will see a good recovery in sales growth, Cui Dongshu, the association's secretary general, told reporters, citing a low base in the corresponding period last year.

feed.title: Indonesia to propose limited free trade deal with US on critical minerals-By Stefanno Sulaiman JAKARTA, April 10 (Reuters) - Indonesia will propose a free trade
feed.summary: Indonesia to propose limited free trade deal with US on critical minerals-By Stefanno Sulaiman JAKARTA, April 10 (Reuters) - Indonesia will propose a free trade agreement for some minerals shipped to the United States so that companies in the electric vehicle battery supply chain operating in the country can benefit from U.S. tax credits, a senior minister said on Monday. Asked about the new IRA guidelines, Indonesian minister Luhut Pandjaitan, who has been spearheading efforts to attract U.S. companies, told a news conference Jakarta will propose a limited free trade agreement (FTA) with Washington. Last month, Ford signed an agreement with an Indonesian unit of Brazilian nickel miner Vale and China's Zhejiang Huayou Cobalt to partner in a $4.5 billion HPAL plant in Indonesia's Sulawesi island.

feed.title: Indonesia to propose limited free trade deal with US on critical minerals-JAKARTA, April 10 (Reuters) - Indonesia will propose a free trade agreement for some m
feed.summary: Indonesia to propose limited free trade deal with US on critical minerals-JAKARTA, April 10 (Reuters) - Indonesia will propose a free trade agreement for some minerals shipped to the United States so that companies in the electric vehicle battery supply chain operating in the country can benefit from U.S. tax credits, a senior minister said on Monday. Washington has issued a new guidance for EV tax credits under the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA), requiring a certain value of battery components to be produced or assembled in North America or a free trade partner. Asked about the new IRA guidelines, Indonesian minister Luhut Pandjaitan, who has been spearheading efforts to attract U.S. companies, told a news conference Jakarta will propose a limited free trade agreement (FTA) with Washington.

Washington has issued a new guidance for EV tax credits under the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA), requiring a certain value of battery components to be produced or assembled in North America or a free trade partner. Asked about the new IRA guidelines, Indonesian minister Luhut Pandjaitan, who has been spearheading efforts to attract U.S. companies, told a news conference Jakarta will propose a limited free trade agreement (FTA) with Washington.

feed.summary: US STOCKS-Wall Street drops as job gains fuel rate-hike worries-Tesla Inc TSLA.O fell 4.2% after the electric-vehicle maker cut prices in the United States between 2% and nearly 6%, a move that analysts cautioned could hurt profitability. By Sruthi Shankar and Ankika Biswas April 10 (Reuters) - U.S. stock indexes fell on Monday on growing concerns that the Federal Reserve will continue to raise interest rates after Friday's jobs data pointed to a resilient labor market. Several economic indicators last week, including weak private payrolls and job openings data, had initially raised hopes of a pause to the market-punishing rate hikes amid the recent banking sector turmoil.
